title: alstroemeria garaventae subsp. longaviensis
intro: Alstroemeria garaventae subsp. longaviensis, commonly known as the Longaví Peruvian Lily or Longaví Lily, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Alstroemeriaceae, native to the Andes mountains in Chile and Peru. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Alstroemeria garaventae subsp. longaviensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
